Notice Of Motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Gravel Company, Inc. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person(S) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition, And To Produce Documents At Deposition
Off Calendar Jun-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCE
James Nevin, Esq., appeared for Plaintiff and moving party: Frank Simpson, Margaret Simpson, Patricia Simpson, George Simpson
John Moran, Esq., appeared for defendant and moving party: San Francisco Gravel Com, Inc.

RULING:
HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Plaintiffs Frank Simpson, Margaret Simpson, Patricia Simpson, George Simpson's motion To Compel Defendant San Francisco Gravel Company, Inc. To Produce Its Custodian Of Records And Person(s) Most Knowledgeable For Deposition, And To Produce Documents At Deposition is denied. Professional Career College, Magna Institute, Inc. V Superior Court (1989) 207 Cal.App.3d 490, 494: 'In the context of this case it would be an absurdity to say that a party who fails to meet the time limits of section 2030 may avoid the consequences of his delay and lack of diligence by propounding the same questions again.' The same analysis has been applied to requests for production of documents in Carter v Superior Court (1990) 218 Cal.App.3d 994. The same analysis must be applied in this instance. All requests for sanctions are denied. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. MATTER NOT REPORTED
